questions.text: One can delegate everything
It is difficult to find someone to whom one can delegate, because most people do not take up responsibilities
To delegate is to cede some of one's power and responsibilities to a team member
It is only fair to delegate the least motivating activities as a priority
Delegation presupposes a climate of trust between the manager and the team member
Every delegation implies a control
Delegation often leads to laxity and to reduced sense of responsibility
Delegation is never final
Sometimes you need to know how to impose delegation on reluctant team members
An employee can refuse to take on a delegated task
Delegating implies that an employee has the right to make mistakes
Delegating is usually pointless, because why have someone else do something you are good at or can do much faster?
The recipient of a delegation controls the task and the result themselves
The concept of control is contrary to the spirit of the delegation approach
The procedures and frequency of checks are established and announced at the start of delegation
Delegation allows the hierarchical manager to better manage his/her time
The manager who delegates too much risks finding one day ... that he/she doesn't have much more to do
In the event of serious unforeseen circumstances in the context of a delegation, the team member informs the manager
When delegating, the manager should not intervene, unless in the control procedures foreseen in advance or if requested by the team member him/herself
In practice, delegation occurs mainly in the absence of a manager

answers.text: You are probably struggling with delegation and the group session should help you see things more clearly.
You already delegate regularly, but there is probably room for progress, so you can motivate your teams and manage priorities for yourself.
You are a champion of delegation! We look forward to sharing your experience during the group session.
